title: DataVault: The State-Management Micro-Library
description: Build a tiny, lightweight state management system from scratch that mimics a simplified version of Zustand or Redux. You will implement a store that handles state updates, notifications, and deep cloning to prevent state mutation.
requirements: A Store class that can hold a state object.
A 'setState' method that updates the state and triggers a callback for all listeners.
Use the spread operator to ensure state is updated immutably.
Implement a 'getState' method to retrieve current values.
Create a 'subscribe' method that allows components to react to state changes.
Implement a 'deepClone' utility to handle nested object state updates without mutating the original.

milestones.title: Immutable Updates & Subscription
tasks: Build the setState method using spread operators
Implement a listeners array to track subscribers
Ensure setState notifies all subscribers upon update
Update setState to use deepClone for nested objects
Add test cases to verify that original state is never mutated

stretchGoals: Implement 'middleware' that logs every state change to the console.
Add a 'commit' method that allows batching multiple state updates together.
Create a simple React wrapper hook `useStore` that connects the vanilla store to React components.

evaluationRubric.immutability: Does updating a nested property in the state affect the previous state object?
evaluationRubric.reactivity: Do all subscribers fire correctly when the state changes?
evaluationRubric.codeQuality: Is the store logic decoupled from the usage example?
